about.title: About Kebumen
about.subtitle: Get to know Kebumen Regency better, a hidden gem on the south coast of Central Java

geography.label: Geography & Location
geography.title: Kebumen Regency
geography.description1: Kebumen Regency is a regency located in Central Java Province, Indonesia. The regency is known as the "City of Swifts" and has an area covering lowland coasts to karst mountains.
geography.description2: Located on the south coast of Java Island, Kebumen has a coastline of approximately 20 km bordering the Indian Ocean. To the north, the regency is surrounded by limestone (karst) mountains which are one of the largest geopark areas in Southeast Asia.

geopark.label: Natural Wealth
geopark.title: Gombong Selatan Karst Geopark
geopark.description1: Kebumen Regency has an extraordinary Gombong Selatan karst area. With approximately 365 caves and 982 karst hills, this area is one of the largest in the world and has been recognized as a UNESCO Global Geopark.
geopark.description2: Limestone formations that have formed over millions of years create spectacular scenery that is home to various endemic flora and fauna, as well as holding many mysteries and interesting legends.

caves.title: 365 Karst Caves
caves.description: The Gombong Selatan karst area has about 365 caves waiting to be explored, making Kebumen a paradise for speleologists and adventure lovers.

beach.title: 20 KM Coastline
beach.description: Beautiful beaches on the southern coast with exotic black sand and waves suitable for surfing and other water sports.

history.title: Long History
history.description: Gua Jatijajar was discovered in 1802 and holds various legendary stories about Raden Kamandaka and Nyi Roro Kidul.

cta.title: Ready to Explore Kebumen?
cta.subtitle: Discover natural beauty, cultural richness, and warmth of Kebumen people in an unforgettable travel experience.

footer.tagline: Official tourism portal of Kebumen Regency, Central Java. Discover stunning natural beauty and cultural heritage.
